title: mvxworld.art
fullDescriptionHtml:
mvxworld.art is a personal identity hub and living archive. not a portfolio, but a world. Each page is a room discovered rather than a section bolted on. The site grows organically, page by page, as a transmission from a multi-handle internet native who treats design, code, and culture as one practice.

What the project is showing

  • Custom design system: A fully hand-rolled CSS token system with a dark archive aesthetic. ink base (#0a0a09), acid green (#c8ff00), and a Cormorant Garamond × IBM Plex Mono type pairing
  • Transmissions engine: A manifest-driven post system where adding new content is two paste steps and a push. no CMS, no build step
  • World architecture: Six live rooms (index, lore, work, gallery, transmissions archive, individual posts) each breaking one design rule as the accent
  • No framework: Vanilla HTML, CSS, and JS. a deliberate constraint that keeps the site fast and fully in control

media.caption: The main world — dark, atmospheric, five rooms

collections.title: The World
collections.description: mvxworld.art runs on a custom design system with one rule: every room breaks one convention as the accent. The result is a site that feels intentional without feeling corporate.

title: KAAR
fullDescriptionHtml:
KAAR is a multiplayer touch-arena party app prototype built in Expo React Native. Everyone places a finger on screen, the room locks in, and a dramatic reveal picks the outcome. The core loop is tactile setup, social suspense, and an exaggerated reveal. then instant replay.

What the project is showing

  • Product thinking from scratch: Full brief, feature map, UX flow, design system, and architecture. all defined before a line of code
  • Multi-touch engineering: Custom touch arena tracking 1–8 simultaneous finger positions with fair and chaos selection modes kept separate in code
  • Design system: Custom tokens (electricLime, hyperCoral, laserBlue), theme switching between prototype and collab modes, reduced-motion awareness
  • Shipped features: Game modes, scoreboard, dare prompts, haptics, session history, local persistence, original sound pack, PWA-style offline behavior

collections.title: App Architecture
collections.description: KAAR was built around a strict separation of fair randomness and chaos logic — ensuring transparent, auditable game outcomes in fair mode with no hidden manipulation.

title: WeekPlanner
fullDescriptionHtml:
WeekPlanner is a structured week-planning app with hourly time blocks, assignees, thought threads, and real-time sync. Built as a full-stack production app with automated cron sync and a PWA setup. not a tutorial project.

What the project is showing

  • Full-stack architecture: Next.js frontend, Supabase backend with migrations, RLS policies, and an automated hourly sync cron via Vault refresh
  • Feature depth: Hour blocks, assignee support, thought thread inbox, end-of-week date logic, week-over-week navigation
  • Production setup: CodeQL security scanning, automated deployment workflows, PWA icons, proper TypeScript across the stack
  • Design quality: Clean, opinionated UI. mockups were built before development, then matched exactly in the final product

media.alt: WeekPlanner app — hourly time blocks and thought threads
media.caption: Week view with hour blocks and thought thread inbox

collections.title: App Overview
collections.description: WeekPlanner was designed mockup-first: the full UI was sketched in HTML before any backend work started. The result is a product where design and implementation stayed in sync throughout.

title: Tainment
fullDescriptionHtml:
Tainment is a Discord bot ecosystem built across two repos. a Python discord.py bot with casino mechanics, fishing games, and subscription management, and a Node.js companion (VibeBit) with trivia, leaderboards, and community features. Built because it should exist, not because it was assigned.

What the project is showing

  • Systems thinking: Two separate bots with overlapping community use cases, each optimized for its runtime (Python for the casino/economy layer, Node.js for real-time community features)
  • Feature breadth: Casino (roulette, slots), fishing with XP and leaderboards, trivia, birthday tracking, admin subscriptions, automod, welcome messages
  • Persistent data: SQLite and JSON-based data layers, XP systems, member tracking, guild-level configuration
  • Automation mentality: Built to run unsupervised. the kind of project that shows how I think about systems, not just interfaces

title: Urban Unleashed
fullDescriptionHtml:
Urban Unleashed is an interface-led landing page concept where visual atmosphere, motion, and interaction work together. The project is less about showing raw technology and more about how a bold digital first impression can still feel usable.

What the project is showing

  • Landing-page composition: Hero framing, visual focus, and a strong first-screen impact
  • Immersive interface thinking: Motion and 3D styling used to support the brand feel instead of overwhelming it
  • Responsive execution: The concept is built to stay visually coherent across screen sizes

collections.title: Lead Interface
collections.description: The main homescreen composition used to introduce the project.

title: PopFusion
fullDescriptionHtml:
PopFusion is an experimental music visualizer built as an expressive web interface. The goal is to make sound feel visual without losing control, readability, or interaction clarity.

What the project is showing

  • Audio-reactive behavior: Motion and visuals respond to sound input
  • Expressive UI: Interface elements feel energetic without turning chaotic
  • Creative coding: The project sits between visual experimentation and front-end interaction design

collections.title: Screen Preview
collections.description: A cover image showing the visual tone of the interface.

title: Iris van Herpen Exhibition
fullDescriptionHtml:
Iris van Herpen Exhibition is a museum concept built around Metamorfose. The project carries one visual language across posters, flyers, and social assets, with a more fashion-led and curated tone throughout.

Why the work belongs together

  • One identity system: The same visual language holds across posters, flyers, and social formats
  • Strong format shifts: Wide, portrait, and square assets still feel connected
  • Consistent atmosphere: The campaign reads as one exhibition world instead of separate pieces

title: The North Face Digital Campaign
fullDescriptionHtml:
The North Face Digital Campaign is shown here as a focused interface rollout rather than a mixed archive. The project is strongest as a set of mobile screens and CRM-style touchpoints built around product visibility, promotional hierarchy, and a consistent retail look.

Why the work belongs together

  • Retail consistency: Product promotion, navigation, and campaign messaging keep the same brand direction throughout the set
  • Mobile-first layout: The strongest assets focus on phone-sized screens, product grids, and supporting campaign pages
  • CRM support: The email piece shows how the same campaign language carries into a direct customer touchpoint

media.alt: The North Face campaign homescreen concept
media.caption: Campaign homescreen concept

collections.title: Mobile Campaign Screens
collections.description: The mobile views that carry the campaign through product promotion, brand storytelling, and supporting utility screens.

collections.title: CRM Email Touchpoint
collections.description: A supporting email layout that adapts the campaign into a more direct product and update channel.

title: Hidden Realms Fest
fullDescriptionHtml:
Hidden Realms Fest is the separate festival project that was previously mixed into the North Face archive. It works best as one event-world collection: identity boards, social launch assets, and motion teasers all built around the same festival look and tone.

Why the work belongs together

  • Identity planning: The moodboard and style sheet define the atmosphere before the launch assets appear
  • Campaign rollout: Social posts and motion teasers extend the same identity into promotional formats
  • Event-world consistency: Typography, color, and imagery stay connected across still and moving pieces
